summary: Defines an overfit/contamination gate that separates untouched first-run validation, same-answer measurement corrections, same-set targeted retries, diagnostic-only probes, and final performance evidence.

outputs.description: Worker contract containing the overfit gate proposal.
Human-readable companion proposal for contamination checks, reuse rules, labels, and claim evidence.

accepted_fields_or_changes.name: result_label_taxonomy
accepted_fields_or_changes.reason: The local logs show first-run, remeasurement, targeted retry, and diagnostic rows being easy to confuse unless every row carries an explicit claim-scope label.

accepted_fields_or_changes.name: set_reuse_rules
proposal: questions_v2_tune.json remains tuning evidence only; 95.55 may be called a tuned champion score, not final validation.
questions_v2_holdout.json is spent as strict holdout after failure inspection and targeted fixes; 81.55 remains first strict holdout evidence and 96.80 is same-holdout targeted-fix remeasurement.
questions_v2 probe subsets are diagnostic only and cannot support final claims.

questions_v3_validation.json first raw run L0 is first validation evidence; L1 is same-answer measurement correction; later v3-driven top-k, alias, qv3_010, adaptive, or prompt work carries overfit_risk until tested on new untouched validation.
accepted_fields_or_changes.reason: These rules match the red-team corrections and v3 loop labels recorded in experiment_log.md, next_improvement_tasks.md, loop_points.csv, and loop_report.md.

accepted_fields_or_changes.name: contamination_checks
proposal: Maintain a set/case exposure registry keyed by question_set path and question ids.
Mark a set exposed once case answers, retrieval chunks, judge reasons, manual review, or failure causes are inspected.

Flag same-set contamination when retrieval aliases, org filters, backfill, source pruning, adaptive top_k, prompts, abstention taxonomy, judge context, or rubric sensitivity are changed after viewing that set.
Flag leaderboard contamination when no-judge/missing-judge rows enter ranked graphs or recomputed rows overwrite first-run evidence.
accepted_fields_or_changes.reason: The repo already encountered same-holdout targeted fixes, abstention metric corrections, no-judge diagnostic rows, and judge-context changes.

accepted_fields_or_changes.name: final_claim_evidence_policy
proposal: Final performance claims require a first full judged run on an untouched validation set after the candidate is frozen.
Judge calibration with planted hallucinated, contradicted, and wrong-document answers must pass before near-ceiling judge scores are trusted.
Allowed evidence must include complete judge fields plus retrieval metrics, latency, exact config, run id, and question set id.
No-judge probes, dry runs, fake-client checks, source diagnostics, calibration-pack creation, and same-set retries are not final performance evidence.
accepted_fields_or_changes.reason: The v3 report explicitly states L4-L8/L11-L13 are local diagnostics or candidates, and L9-L10 are no-judge targeted probes, not performance scores.

rejected_or_risky_items.item: Treating v2 holdout post-fix 96.80 as strict held-out/generalization performance
rejected_or_risky_items.risk: The same 7-case holdout failures were inspected and used for targeted fixes before the rerun.

rejected_or_risky_items.item: Replacing the original v2 first holdout 81.55 with regenerated or changed-rule summaries
rejected_or_risky_items.risk: This hides the first strict generalization warning signal.

rejected_or_risky_items.item: Using no-judge or local diagnostic rows in ranked EDD comparisons
rejected_or_risky_items.risk: Rows missing groundedness/relevance are not comparable to fully judged runs.

rejected_or_risky_items.item: Claiming v3 post-L1 improvements as fresh validation
rejected_or_risky_items.risk: The v3 set has been used for targeted top-k, alias/filter, qv3_010, adaptive guard, quality review, and prompt candidate work.

rejected_or_risky_items.item: Accepting near-ceiling judge scores before blind calibration
rejected_or_risky_items.risk: Logs note possible balanced-judge leniency and a need to catch planted wrong-document/hallucinated answers.

needs_orchestrator_review.question: Where should the exposure registry live and which script should attach these labels to scoreboard rows?
needs_orchestrator_review.suggestion: Have the orchestrator decide final file ownership, because this worker is proposal-only and cannot edit scripts or shared eval files.

needs_orchestrator_review.question: Should new v3 splits be considered regression sets only or can an untouched subset be salvaged?
needs_orchestrator_review.suggestion: Default to regression-only unless the orchestrator can prove the split/cases were not inspected or used for any candidate decision.

needs_orchestrator_review.question: What minimum size/diversity is required for the new untouched validation set?
needs_orchestrator_review.suggestion: At minimum include compare, ambiguous-org, follow-up, precise-value, abstention, source-scope bleed, and false-positive backfill stress cases; avoid precise population claims from very small sets.

commands_run: PowerShell ConvertFrom-Json attempt on eval/questions_v2*.json and eval/questions_v3_validation.json
observed_results: Prior v3 loop artifacts were present: loop_points.csv and loop_report.md.
v2 tune, holdout, compare, groundedness, and holdout-failure probe files were present; v3 validation file was present.

loop_points.csv labels L0 as first raw validation 89.69, L1 as same-answer measurement correction 96.36, L2/L3 top5 as rejected global default, and L4-L13 as diagnostics/probes/candidates rather than final scores.
experiment_log.md records 95.55 as tuned-set champion, 81.55 as first strict holdout warning, and 96.80 as same-holdout targeted-fix remeasurement rather than strict held-out score.
next_improvement_tasks.md says a new untouched validation set is still required and existing v2 holdout is no longer strict held-out evidence.
Direct PowerShell JSON parsing of question files failed due local encoding/escaping issues, so the proposal relies on logs and visible file content rather than parsed question metadata.
